message.content:
I need to understand the structure of the RBSA-SpecTop.adoc file to know where to insert a new operation. Please:

1. Read /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc and identify:
   - All section headings (== and ===) with their line numbers
   - All include:: directives with their line numbers
   - All operation anchors (lines starting with [[rbtgo_)
   - The linked term attributes used with `rbhg_` prefix (human guide behavioral annotations)
   - The pattern used for operation headings (how are operations titled?)

2. Read /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBSPE-payor_establish.adoc to understand the exact pattern used for human-guide procedures

3. Report the findings so I can create a new operation spec that fits seamlessly into the existing structure.

content.text: I'll read these files to understand the structure and patterns.

content.content:
1→The
     2→{at_operator}
     3→performs one-time manual setup via Google Cloud Console to establish
     4→{rbtr_payor}
     5→OAuth infrastructure.
     6→No API path exists for initial bootstrap - OAuth client creation requires human Console interaction.
     7→This procedure creates the project and OAuth client but does NOT install credentials.
     8→
     9→// ⟦axs_inputs axd_none⟧
    10→
    11→// ⟦axs_behavior⟧
    12→
    13→. *Create Payor Project*:
    14→.. Navigate to project creation in Console
    15→.. Set project name: `Recipe Bottle Payor`
    16→.. Set project ID: `rbw-payor` (preferred fixed name, single payor per account)
    17→.. If `rbw-payor` already exists, use `rbw-payor-[4-digit-suffix]` (e.g., rbw-payor-2024)
    18→.. {rbhg_store}
    19→actual project ID as `«PAYOR_PROJECT_ID»` for subsequent operations
    20→.. If Console displays "You need additional access" error after project creation, wait a few minutes and refresh the page.
    21→GCP IAM changes are https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/access-change-propagation[eventually consistent] and may take time to propagate.
    22→
    23→. *Configure Billing*:
    24→.. Access billing management in Console
    25→.. Select or create billing account
    26→.. Record
    27→{rbrp_billing_account_id}
    28→(format: XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX)
    29→.. Link billing account to payor project
    30→.. {rbhg_require}
    31→billing linkage for API operations
    32→
    33→. *Enable Required APIs*:
    34→.. Navigate to APIs & Services > Enable APIs
    35→.. Enable: Cloud Resource Manager, Cloud Billing, Service Usage, IAM, Artifact Registry
    36→.. These enable programmatic depot management
    37→
    38→. *Configure OAuth Consent Screen*:
    39→.. Navigate to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen
    40→.. Click "Get started" to launch Project Configuration wizard
    41→.. Complete 4-step wizard:
    42→... Step 1 - App Information: Set app name and user support email
    43→... Step 2 - Audience: Select "External" (testing mode, no verification required)
    44→... Step 3 - Contact Information: Provide email for notifications
    45→... Step 4 - Finish: Accept User Data Policy and create
    46→
    47→. *Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID*:
    48→.. Navigate to APIs & Services > Credentials
    49→.. Create credentials > OAuth client ID
    50→.. Application type: Desktop app
    51→.. Name: "Recipe Bottle Payor"
    52→.. Click CREATE
    53→.. Popup displays client ID and client secret
    54→.. Download JSON (filename: `client_secret_[id].apps.googleusercontent.com.json`)
    55→.. {rbhg_warn}
    56→Client secret visible only in this popup; June 2025 onwards, Google will not display it after closing
    57→
    58→// ⟦axs_outputs⟧
    59→
    60→The outputs of payor establishment are:
    61→- Project ID (`«PAYOR_PROJECT_ID»`) recorded from step 1
    62→- OAuth client credentials: client ID and secret from the downloaded JSON file
    63→
    64→// ⟦axs_completion⟧
    65→
    66→NOTE: Payor establishment creates the OAuth client configuration but does not perform credential installation.
    67→Proceed to
    68→{rbtgo_payor_install}
    69→to complete setup by installing and testing OAuth authentication.
    70→
File content (30188 tokens) exceeds maximum allowed tokens (25000). Please use offset and limit parameters to read specific portions of the file, or use the GrepTool to search for specific content.

content.text: Perfect! Now let me verify the structure around operations and understand the pattern better by looking at a few more operation files:

input.description: Show operation header pattern in RBSA-SpecTop.adoc

content.content:
1→The
     2→{at_operator}
     3→completes OAuth credential installation by running the automated installation command with the downloaded OAuth client JSON file.
     4→This step performs the OAuth authorization flow, securely stores credentials, and validates the Payor project is accessible.
     5→
     6→// ⟦axs_inputs⟧
     7→. *Prerequisites*:
     8→.. {rbhg_require}
     9→{rbrp_billing_account_id}
    10→is set in the current shell environment
    11→.. {rbhg_require}
    12→OAuth client JSON file from
    13→{rbtgo_payor_establish}
    14→is available locally
    15→
    16→// ⟦axs_behavior⟧
    17→. *Execute OAuth Credential Installation*:
    18→.. Export
    19→{rbrp_billing_account_id}
    20→to environment (obtained from {rbtgo_payor_establish} step 2)
    21→.. Run installation command with path to downloaded OAuth JSON:
    22→+
    23→[source,bash]
    24→----
    25→export RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID=<your-billing-account-id>
    26→rbgp_payor_install ~/Downloads/client_secret_*.json
    27→----
    28→
    29→.. Script validates OAuth JSON file structure
    30→.. Script performs OAuth authorization flow:
    31→... Opens browser to Google OAuth consent screen
    32→... User clicks "Continue" on unverified app warning
    33→... User grants Recipe Bottle Payor access to cloud resources
    34→... Google displays authorization code
    35→... User copies and pastes authorization code to terminal prompt
    36→
    37→. *Credential Storage*:
    38→.. OAuth credentials stored securely in `~/.rbw/rbro.env` with 600 permissions
    39→.. Credentials include:
    40→... `RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET`: OAuth client secret for token refresh
    41→... `RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN`: Long-lived refresh token for authentication
    42→.. {rbhg_warn}
    43→Never commit `~/.rbw/rbro.env` to version control
    44→
    45→// ⟦axs_outputs⟧
    46→. *Configuration Summary*:
    47→.. Installation displays payor configuration values needed in `rbrp.env`:
    48→... `RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID`: Payor project ID from OAuth JSON
    49→... `RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`: OAuth client ID from OAuth JSON
    50→... `RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID`: Already provided before installation
    51→
    52→. *Next Steps*:
    53→.. Record configuration values for `rbrp.env` setup
    54→.. Use {rbtgo_depot_create} to create depot projects
    55→.. Example: `rbgp_depot_create dev us-central1`
    56→
    57→// ⟦axs_completion⟧
    58→Successful execution stores OAuth credentials and displays payor configuration values.
    59→

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings:

## Findings Report: RBSA-SpecTop.adoc Structure and Patterns

Based on my analysis of the files, here's the complete structural information for creating a new operation spec:

### 1. SECTION HEADINGS STRUCTURE

**Top-level operational sections (== level):**
- Line 632: `== {rbtr_payor} Operations`
- Line 680: `== {rbtr_governor} Operations`
- Line 694: `== {rbtr_director} Operations`
- Line 742: `== {rbtr_retriever} Operations`
- Line 758: `== Multi Role Operations`
- Line 772: `== Local Operations`
- Line 849: `== VM Management Operations`

**Operation entries (=== level):**
All operations use the pattern:
```
[[rbtgo_anchor_name]]

=== {rbtgo_anchor_name}

include::FILE-acronym.adoc[]
```

### 2. OPERATION ANCHOR PATTERN

All operation anchors follow this format:
```
[[rbtgo_OPERATION_NAME]]
```

Examples from RBSA-SpecTop.adoc:
- `[[rbtgo_payor_establish]]` (line 634)
- `[[rbtgo_payor_install]]` (line 640)
- `[[rbtgo_payor_refresh]]` (line 648)
- `[[rbtgo_depot_create]]` (line 654)
- `[[rbtgo_depot_destroy]]` (line 662)
- `[[rbtgo_depot_list]]` (line 668)

### 3. LINKED TERM ATTRIBUTES (rbhg_ prefix)

The human guide behavioral annotations use the `rbhg_` prefix:

| Attribute | Display Value | Definition |
|-----------|---------------|-----------|
| `:rbhg_warn:` | **CAUTION** | Warning/cautionary note (line 343) |
| `:rbhg_show:` | **NOTE** | Informational note (line 344) |
| `:rbhg_store:` | **RECORD** | Action to record/capture (line 345) |
| `:rbhg_fatal:` | **STOP** | Critical stopping point (line 346) |
| `:rbhg_require:` | **VERIFY** | Verification requirement (line 347) |

### 4. OPERATION HEADING PATTERN

From RBSPE-payor_establish.adoc analysis, operation specifications follow this consistent format:

**Structure of each operation file:**
```
The {linked_term} performs [action description].

// ⟦axs_inputs⟧

. *Prerequisites*:
.. {rbhg_require} [requirement]
.. {rbhg_require} [requirement]

// ⟦axs_behavior⟧

. *[Step Title]*:
.. [Step details with indentation]
.. Code examples in [source,bash] blocks
.. {rbhg_warn}, {rbhg_show}, {rbhg_store}, {rbhg_require} as needed

// ⟦axs_outputs⟧

. *[Output Title]*:
.. Output details

// ⟦axs_completion⟧

[Completion note with potential cross-reference to next operation using {rbtgo_*}]
```

### 5. INCLUDE DIRECTIVE PATTERN

The include directives in RBSA-SpecTop.adoc follow this exact pattern (lines 638, 646, 652, etc.):

```
include::ACRONYM-operation_name.adoc[]
```

**Examples:**
- `include::RBSPE-payor_establish.adoc[]` (line 638)
- `include::RBSPI-payor_install.adoc[]` (line 646)
- `include::RBSPR-payor_refresh.adoc[]` (line 652)

### 6. OPERATION GROUPING

Operations are grouped by role/responsibility:

| Section | Operations | Lines |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Payor Operations | establish, install, refresh | 632-678 |
| Governor Operations | retriever_create, director_create | 680-692 |
| Director Operations | trigger_build, image_list, image_delete, ark_abjure, ark_beseech, ark_conjure | 694-740 |
| Retriever Operations | image_retrieve, ark_summon | 742-756 |
| Multi-Role | sa_list, sa_delete | 758-770 |

### 7. OPERATION MAPPING ATTRIBUTES

The operation anchors are mapped at the top of RBSA-SpecTop.adoc (lines 77-102):

```
:rbtgo_operation_name:  <<rbtgo_operation_name,display_function_name>>
```

**Example:**
```
:rbtgo_payor_establish:  <<rbtgo_payor_establish,rbgm_payor_establish>>
```

The mapping enables:
1. Creating linked references: `{rbtgo_operation_name}`
2. Linking to the operation section: `{rbtgo_operation_name}`

---

## Pattern Summary for Creating RBSQB (quota_build)

When creating your new operation, you'll need to:

1. **Define the anchor** in the mapping section (~line 100):
   ```
   :rbtgo_quota_build:    <<rbtgo_quota_build,function_name>>
   ```

2. **Add to operation section** (determines which role section to place it in):
   ```
   [[rbtgo_quota_build]]

   === {rbtgo_quota_build}

   include::RBSQB-quota_build.adoc[]
   ```

3. **Create RBSQB-quota_build.adoc** with the structure:
   - Opening description with `{at_operator}` or relevant role reference
   - `// ⟦axs_inputs⟧` section with prerequisites using `{rbhg_require}`
   - `// ⟦axs_behavior⟧` section with numbered steps and control terms
   - `// ⟦axs_outputs⟧` section listing outputs
   - `// ⟦axs_completion⟧` section with cross-reference to next operation using `{rbtgo_*}`

This structure ensures seamless integration with the existing documentation pattern.
